---
title: "Regression"
subtitle: "The workhorse of applied economics, used carefully"
---

## Overview

Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) regression is the most widely used tool in applied economics. Understanding its mechanics --- what it estimates, under what assumptions, and how it can mislead --- is essential before moving to more advanced causal designs.

This module covers the geometry and algebra of OLS, the Frisch-Waugh-Lovell theorem, the anatomy of omitted variable bias, interaction terms, functional form choices, and inference.

## Pages in This Module

- [OLS Mechanics](ols-mechanics.qmd) --- What OLS does geometrically and algebraically.
- [Frisch-Waugh-Lovell](frisch-waugh-lovell.qmd) --- Partialling out and the anatomy of multivariate regression.
- [Omitted Variable Bias](omitted-variable-bias.qmd) --- The bias formula and its implications for research design.
- [Interactions](interactions.qmd) --- Heterogeneous effects and how to model them.
- [Functional Form](functional-form.qmd) --- Logs, polynomials, and choosing the right specification.
- [Inference](inference.qmd) --- Standard errors, clustering, and hypothesis testing in regression.
